message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Re-review: blocking items fixed, CI green">
Luca has addressed all blocking review items and CI is green. Reviewers who had blocking items — please re-review to verify your concerns are resolved:

- Steve Krug, Lea Verou, Steve Schoger: context headings/grouping
- Carson Gross: filter partial swap
- Heydon Pickering: focus management + visible label on context select
- Marty Cagan: your 2 blocking items

If satisfied, please send your consensus approval to luca-palmieri. Kent Beck and Scott Wlaschin already approved.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Let me re-read the updated files to verify the blocking item (context grouping headings in the "All" view) has been addressed.

Let me also check the route handler to verify it's sending grouped data.

content.text:
The implementation addresses my blocking item well. Let me verify the design specifics:

**Template structure** -- The "All" view now uses `<section>` elements with `<h2>` headings per context group, wrapped in `aria-labelledby`. The filtered single-context view correctly omits the headings and uses a flat list. This is exactly what I asked for.

**CSS for the heading** -- Let me check the heading color. I see it uses `--color-fg-muted` rather than `--color-fg` which I had recommended. Let me consider whether this is actually a problem or acceptable.

Looking at it more carefully: the context headings at `--font-size-lg` / `--font-semibold` / `--color-fg-muted` will create a clear structural hierarchy. Using `--color-fg-muted` (gray-600, ~7:1 contrast) rather than `--color-fg` (gray-800) is actually a reasonable choice here -- it prevents the context headings from competing with the page `<h1>` "Next Actions" for visual primacy. The headings are structural dividers, not the primary focal point. The semibold weight provides enough distinction. I can accept this.

The `margin-block-end: var(--space-lg)` on `.next-actions__group` provides good separation between groups. The heading gets `margin-block-end: var(--space-sm)` before items. This matches my recommended spacing.

I'm satisfied. Let me send my consensus approval.

input.content:
Steve Schoger here -- re-review complete. My blocking item is resolved.

**Verification of the fix:**
- The "All" view now correctly groups actions by context with `<h2>` headings inside `<section>` elements (`templates/next_actions.html:67-77`). This matches the US-GTD-3 acceptance criteria for "context headings."
- The filtered single-context view correctly omits the headings and uses a flat list (`templates/next_actions.html:78-86`). Smart decision.
- CSS for `.next-actions__context-heading` uses `--font-size-lg` / `--font-semibold` / `--color-fg-muted` -- I originally suggested `--color-fg`, but `--color-fg-muted` at 7:1 contrast is actually the right call here. It creates structural grouping without competing with the page `<h1>` for visual primacy.
- Group spacing with `margin-block-end: var(--space-lg)` on `.next-actions__group` and `margin-block-end: var(--space-sm)` on the heading provides clear visual separation between context groups.
- The `build_context_groups()` function in the route handler preserves context order and only includes contexts that have actions, which is the right behavior.

All token compliance remains clean. No new design system violations.

**CONSENSUS: APPROVED** from Steve Schoger (UI Design).
input.summary: APPROVED — blocking item resolved, consensus given

routing.summary: APPROVED — blocking item resolved, consensus given
routing.content:
Steve Schoger here -- re-review complete. My blocking item is resolved.

**Verification of the fix:**
- The "All" view now correctly groups actions by context with `<h2>` headings inside `<section>` elements (`templates/next_actions.html:67-77`). This matches the US-GTD-3 acceptance criteria for "context headings."
- The filtered single-context view correctly omits the headings and uses a flat list (`templates/next_actions.html:78-86`). Smart decision.
- CSS for `.next-actions__context-heading` uses `--font-size-lg` / `--font-semibold` / `--color-fg-muted` -- I originally suggested `--color-fg`, but `--color-fg-muted` at 7:1 contrast is actually the right call here. It creates structural grouping without competing with the page `<h1>` for visual primacy.
- Group spacing with `margin-block-end: var(--space-lg)` on `.next-actions__group` and `margin-block-end: var(--space-sm)` on the heading provides clear visual separation between context groups.
- The `build_context_groups()` function in the route handler preserves context order and only includes contexts that have actions, which is the right behavior.

All token compliance remains clean. No new design system violations.

**CONSENSUS: APPROVED** from Steve Schoger (UI Design).

content.text:
My blocking item has been resolved. Here's what I verified:

- **Context grouping headings** are now present in the "All" view using `<section>` elements with `<h2>` headings, properly linked with `aria-labelledby`
- The **filtered single-context view** correctly omits redundant headings
- The **CSS styling** for `.next-actions__context-heading` uses appropriate tokens (`--font-size-lg`, `--font-semibold`, `--color-fg-muted`) with proper spacing
- The **route handler** groups actions by context in display order, only showing contexts that have actions
- **Token compliance** remains clean throughout

I've sent my **CONSENSUS: APPROVED** to the Driver.
